ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Son Gohan' — identity is explicit. Vision describes golden SSJ hair, dark outfit, powerful upright stance — canonical Super Saiyan Gohan. Colossal Warfare includes multiple Gohan cards (BT4-006 Blaze of Glory Son Gohan, BT4-048 Newfound Power Son Gohan, BT4-091 Adoptive Father Son Gohan). BT4-028 is a Common-tier Gohan consistent with set design patterns.

trivia_bullets.text: Colossal Warfare (DBS-B04) features at least four distinct Son Gohan cards across different rarities — BT4-006 (Blaze of Glory), BT4-028, BT4-048 (Newfound Power), and BT4-091 (Adoptive Father) — reflecting how the set draws from multiple Dragon Ball arcs simultaneously alongside its Baby Saga primary theme. [Source: Bleeding Cool: Colossal Warfare Checklist; DBZ Exchange product listings]

Son Gohan is the franchise's most prominent half-Saiyan — son of Goku and the human Chi-Chi. His special trait in the DBS card game is listed as Saiyan/Earthling, reflecting his dual heritage. The card game encodes this hybrid identity at the mechanical level through trait combinations not available to pure-Saiyan characters. [Source: WebSearch snippet, DBS card game card lists]

Gohan's 'hidden power' — a latent potential consistently higher than any pure-Saiyan peer — is a recurring narrative device across DBZ and DBS. His Mystic/Ultimate form (unlocked by Elder Kai in the Buu Saga) surpassed SS3 Goku without a traditional SSJ transformation, making him a design outlier in a franchise built on transformation escalation. [Source: GameRant: All Forms of Gotenks, Explained — context for Buu Saga power ceiling]
